characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Blue Is Heroic: She wears a blue shirt and blue tights. Catchphrase: Quite a few:"Have a sunny day!" - Said whenever someone leaves the salon following an appointment with her."Ready...set...gear up and go!" - Said prior to leaving in the Glam Van, with Doodle, Rox and Blair joining in on the end."This is one for the Style Files!" - Said when snapping a photo of someone in the episode's featured hairstyle."Never say never!" - Normally said to encourage her friends to not give up. characters.name: Rox
characters.description: Big Eater: Whenever Rox is in proximity to cakes, she will immediately express her intent to eat them, even if she should not. It appears she can't go for more then an hour without needing to eat something. Tomboy and Girly Girl: The Tomboy to Blair's Girly Girl. Tomboy with a Girly Streak: Rox is shown to like skateboarding, and is a big eater. She also works at the hair salon and likes watching ballet.

characters.name: Blair
characters.description: Neat Freak: Blair insists on organizing everything in the salon, arranging cosmetic supplies by a color chart and insists on everything being tidy/stored away properly. Tomboy and Girly Girl: The Girly Girl to Rox's Tomboy.

characters.name: Doodle
characters.description: Precious Puppy: Although he's technically an adult dog.

characters.name: Lacey
characters.description: Crocodile Tears: When Lacey thinks she'll be inconvenienced by an event or problem, she'll immediately start sobbing and playing on the sympathies of others. As soon as those people assure her they're not being judgemental, Lacey immediately goes back to being confident and upbeat. Dick Dastardly Stops to Cheat: Whenever she competes against others in competitions, her first thought is to do something to her opponents to make them look bad or ruin their performance/entry. Lampshaded by KC in one episode where she points out if Lacey was actually to put effort into her own performance instead of sabotaging the other competitors, she might stand a better chance of winning fairly. Hidden Depths: Lacey is exceedingly good at fixing anything, from plumbing problems, to broken windows and automobile breakdowns. According to KC, Lacey performs all their home repairs personally. Karma Houdini: Lacey receives almost no punishments for attempting to outshine Sunny. Especially in "Band Together" when she steals Doodle's drum kit and the girls never found out she was the one behind such. Promotion to Opening Titles: Since “Pet Parlor”, she is added to the theme song.

characters.name: KC
characters.description: Only Sane Dog: Frequently points out Lacey's bad ideas and offers good advice, only to be ignored and disregarded. Pink Means Feminine: Has pale pink fur. Promotion to Opening Titles: She appears alongside Lacey in the updated title sequence as of “Pet Parlor”.

characters.name: Secondary Characters
characters.description: The Klutz: Is often prone to accidentally dropping anything she is holding, which turns out to be bad when it's a freshly made cake or delicate dessert. Sibling Yin-Yang: Unlike her snobbish sister Lacey, she's kinder and more supportive. Tomboy and Girly Girl: The Tomboy to Lacey's Girly Girl. Jerk with a Heart of Gold: Despite his insistence on performing his dogcatching duties obsessively, Scratch is not actually a bad person.It comes as a big surprise that Scratch actually has a pet puppy.
